# NativeSelect

A native HTML select component styled with Chakra UI. Uses the browser's built-in dropdown for maximum compatibility and mobile-friendliness.

## Import

```jsx
import { NativeSelect, NativeSelectField, NativeSelectIndicator } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Sub-components

| Component | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `NativeSelect` | The root wrapper (renders `NativeSelect.Root`) |
| `NativeSelectField` | The native `<select>` element with Chakra styling |
| `NativeSelectIndicator` | The dropdown arrow indicator icon |

## NativeSelect (Root) Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `"xs" \| "sm" \| "md" \| "lg"` | `"md"` | The size of the select |
| `variant` | `"outline" \| "subtle" \| "flushed"` | `"outline"` | The visual variant of the select |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the select is disabled |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | - | The NativeSelectField and NativeSelectIndicator |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the root element |

## Usage

### Basic

```jsx
<NativeSelect>
  <NativeSelectField placeholder="Select a country">
    <option value="us">United States</option>
    <option value="uk">United Kingdom</option>
    <option value="in">India</option>
  </NativeSelectField>
  <NativeSelectIndicator />
</NativeSelect>
```

### With size

```jsx
<NativeSelect size="lg">
  <NativeSelectField>
    <option value="sm">Small</option>
    <option value="md">Medium</option>
    <option value="lg">Large</option>
  </NativeSelectField>
  <NativeSelectIndicator />
</NativeSelect>
```

### With Field wrapper

```jsx
<Field label="Country" required>
  <NativeSelect>
    <NativeSelectField placeholder="Select a country">
      <option value="us">United States</option>
      <option value="uk">United Kingdom</option>
    </NativeSelectField>
    <NativeSelectIndicator />
  </NativeSelect>
</Field>
```

## Features

- Uses the browser's native `<select>` element for maximum compatibility
- Better performance on mobile devices compared to custom select dropdowns
- Supports standard HTML `<option>` and `<optgroup>` elements
- Compound component pattern for layout flexibility
- Each sub-component forwards refs to its underlying DOM element
